title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: US and Iran digitally re-sign deal to end war and open Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary:
US and Iran digitally re-sign deal to end war and open Strait of Hormuz. The United States and Iran remotely signed their memorandum of understanding to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz for the second time on Wednesday, the White House told the Washington Examiner.

The signing means Iran will “instantly reopen” the Strait of Hormuz and the U.S. will “immediately lift” its naval blockade on all […]. Al Jazeera: Iran war live: Pakistan says MoU in effect after Trump, Pezeshkian signing.

Al Jazeera reports that uS officials say the deal includes an end to war in Lebanon and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

links.headline: LISTEN: The war is over -- or is it?
links.excerpt: President Trump announced the end of the war with Iran, and said a peace deal was in the process of being signed. A day later, he announced the Strait of Hormuz would soon reopen and that the peace deal was in the process of being signed. But questions remain: Are we at war or not?

stories.title: REPORT: Trump Personally Signs Iran Agreement at Versailles, Memorandum in Effect
stories.summary:
REPORT: Trump Personally Signs Iran Agreement at Versailles, Memorandum in Effect. President Donald Trump personally signed a copy of an agreement aimed at ending the conflict between the United States and Iran during a dinner at the Palace of Versailles, according to a report from Barak Ravid, the global affairs correspondent for Axios and a CNN analyst.

The post REPORT: Trump Personally Signs Iran Agreement at Versa. BBC: Moment Trump signs US-Iran agreement at Palace of Versailles.

BBC reports that the 14-point Memorandum of Understanding was signed at a post-G7 dinner in France.

stories.title: All detainees from immigration facility 'Alligator Alcatraz' have been transferred, DHS says
stories.summary:
All detainees from immigration facility 'Alligator Alcatraz' have been transferred, DHS says. All detainees at an immigration detention center in an isolated airstrip in the Florida Everglades, known as "Alligator Alcatraz," have been transferred to other facilities, the U.S. Department of Homeland Security said, citing concerns related to the hurricane season.

Al Jazeera: US transfers immigrants out of Florida Alligator Alcatraz detention centre. Al Jazeera reports that the facility, located near the Everglade wetlands, has drawn human rights concerns and lawsuits over its conditions.

stories.title: Serial killer who murdered eight women in Long Island town imprisoned for life
stories.summary:
Serial killer who murdered eight women in Long Island town imprisoned for life. In fiery exchange, judge sentenced him to three life sentences plus 25 years to life on four other charges, all running consecutively.

Al Jazeera: Gilgo Beach killer sentenced to life without parole.

stories.title: The Lawsuit That Could Blow Up The Child Sex-Change Industry
stories.summary:
The Lawsuit That Could Blow Up The Child Sex-Change Industry. The federal government is suing an infamous transgender medical activist group and alleging it has deceived the public about the harms of child sex changes to make a profit.

The Federal Trade Commission announced it’s suing the World Professional Association for Transgender Health (WPATH) over allegations the group made “false and unsubstantiated claims” that made ... Washington Times: FTC lawsuit accuses transgender-health group of making false claims to back 'gender-affirming care'.

Washington Times reports that the Federal Trade Commission and four states filed a lawsuit Wednesday against the World Professional Association for Transgender Health, arguing that its "deceptive claims" have provided the basis for harmful gender-transition surgeries and drugs for minors.

stories.title: Mom Who Turned In Son Over UFC White House Plot Shares Heartbreaking Message
stories.summary:
Mom Who Turned In Son Over UFC White House Plot Shares Heartbreaking Message. A concerned mother called authorities over her son’s alleged role in plotting an attack at the UFC Freedom 250 event at the White House over the weekend after she noticed alarming behavior.

Officers responded to the family’s home on the evening of June 10 after the mother, Danielle Proper, called about a “disturbance” involving her ... Daily Wire: The Disturbing Call That Unraveled The UFC White House Terror Plot.

Daily Wire reports that law enforcement officials discovered the alleged plot to attack the UFC Freedom 250 event at the White House over the weekend after a concerned mother called police, according to an FBI criminal complaint.

stories.title: As a Muslim, it is encouraging to hear Pauline Hanson quote from a book. If there’s one thing we need, it’s more reading | Aftab Malik
stories.summary:
As a Muslim, it is encouraging to hear Pauline Hanson quote from a book. If there’s one thing we need, it’s more reading | Aftab Malik.

Her latest remarks drew on Ed Husain’s memoir The Islamist. Reading more may help reframe the debate on Islam, writes Australia’s special envoy to combat Islamophobia I must admit that Senator Pauline Hanson surprised me this week.

But not for the reasons you might think. After years of hearing the senator speak about Muslims, Islam, mosques, immigration and national identity, I never imagined I would see the day when she would stand up and quote from a book.

stories.title: UFC 6 review: a bloody, brilliant MMA fighting game
stories.summary:
UFC 6 review: a bloody, brilliant MMA fighting game. PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S; EA Vancouver/Electronic Arts Micromanaging your fighter is a little tedious, but the action is thrilling in this authentically detailed sporting simulation Becoming a professional fighter takes years of repetition, drilling techniques and training footwork until everything is instinctual.

Your body needs an automatic answer for every limb, from every angle. In MMA, which encompasses every martial art, it’s even harder.

EA Sports’.

stories.title: Inquirer Foundation opens applications for 2026-28 scholarships
stories.summary:
Inquirer Foundation opens applications for 2026-28 scholarships.

MANILA, Philippines — The Inquirer Foundation is now accepting applications for the Inquirer/Aboitiz Scholarship Grant (Batch 2026-2028), a scholarship program designed to help high-achieving but financially challenged students driven to pursue a career in media.

The program supports qualified college students through their junior and senior years by providing vital financial assistance, internship, and mentorship.

stories.title: Pagcor orders licensees, operators to promote gambling helpline
stories.summary:
Pagcor orders licensees, operators to promote gambling helpline. MANILA, Philippines — The Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corp.

(Pagcor) on Wednesday has ordered that all existing advertisements advocating “responsible gaming” be replaced with materials promoting its 24/7 National Problem Gambling Helpline (NPGH).

Citing a June 9 memorandum issued by its Gaming Licensing and Development Department, Pagcor issued the directive to all licensees, suppliers, gaming.

stories.title: PCSO: No jackpot winners in June 17 lotto draws
stories.summary:
PCSO: No jackpot winners in June 17 lotto draws. MANILA, Philippines—No bettor won the jackpot prizes in the Grand Lotto 6/55 and MegaLotto 6/45 draws conducted Wednesday, June 17, according to the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO).

Grand Lotto 6/55 produced the winning combination 55-31-46-37-48-01 for a jackpot worth ₱45 million.

No ticket matched all six numbers. MegaLotto 6/45 yielded the winning combination 34-35-09-10-19-32.

stories.title: Sara Duterte impeachment pre-trial conference commences
stories.summary:
Sara Duterte impeachment pre-trial conference commences. MANILA, Philippines — Vice President Sara Duterte’s defense counsel, and the House of Representatives prosecution panel came face-to-face during the pre-trial for the impeachment of the vice president that convened on Thursday.

Making up the prosecution are Reps. Gerville Reyes-Luistro, Terry Ridon, Ramon Gutierrez, Ysabel Zamora, Lorenz Defensor, Chel Diokno, Leila De Lima, Keith Flores,.

stories.summary: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo's Safe-Haven City. Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

stories.title: Two Stanford grads raise $11M to build a noninvasive wearable for hormone tracking
stories.summary: Two Stanford grads raise $11M to build a noninvasive wearable for hormone tracking. Clair Health will track inflammation and bloating markers, energy levels, and cycle phase classification to give insights into cycle irregularities and perimenopause, as well as hormonal fluctuations, and how to navigate those changes.
